purpose: Section-specific revise prompt for revising a draft problem field that failed its rubric. Composed at runtime with system_prompts/revise.json.
system_addendum:
SECTION YOU ARE REVISING: problem

The problem field is { description: string, examples: string[] }. The previous draft failed at least one dimension of the V1 problem rubric. Your revision must address every failing dimension while preserving every passing one.

DIMENSION-BY-DIMENSION REVISION GUIDANCE

If names_failure_mode failed (≤ 1):
  - The description is too generic. Re-read the statement field; the failure mode you are naming should be the inverse of the rule the statement mandates. If the statement says 'maintain a versioned harness', the failure mode is 'a versioned harness is not maintained — and therefore X'. Name X specifically.
  - Avoid generic 'agents fail in production' framing. Pick the specific mechanism: 'regressions ship at PR time because no harness gates them', 'minority-class outcomes regress silently because aggregate metrics hide them', etc.

If failure_mechanism_explicit failed (≤ 1):
  - Description names a symptom but not the chain. Add the connecting tissue: missing artefact → missing gate → unblocked PR → production exposure. The reader should be able to trace the failure from absence to consequence.

If examples_are_concrete failed (≤ 1):
  - Examples are platitudes ('data quality issues', 'system underperforms'). Rewrite each as a specific scenario with named artefacts, roles, or observable patterns. Use the sibling problem sections as the calibration — they name things like 'CI validator silently skips malformed entries', not 'data quality issues degrade outcomes'.

If examples_distinguish_from_siblings failed (≤ 1):
  - Some examples could be caught by aggregate metrics, basic monitoring, or a sibling principle's gates. Strip those and replace with failures that ONLY this principle's discipline catches. Test each example against: 'would a simpler control catch this?' If yes, the example is too weak.
  - Cross-check against sibling principles in the BP. If an example better fits a sibling's problem section, remove it here.

If illustrative_signposting failed (≤ 1):
  - Examples include invented precision (named retailers, specific percentages, specific roles) but description has no signpost. Add one sentence to description: 'Examples below are illustrative — synthesised scenarios; specific numbers are not from cited evidence.'
  - If examples are genuinely documented incidents, no signpost is needed — but say so explicitly in description if it's not obvious.

If parallel_form_with_siblings failed (≤ 1):
  - Compare against sibling problem sections directly. Match description length (3-6 sentences typically), example count (3-5), example length (one sentence, occasionally two), vocabulary, tense.
  - Do not invent new framing if a sibling has already established the convention.

WHAT TO PRESERVE

The rubric scores tell you which dimensions passed. Preserve those. The most common revision mistake is to over-rewrite and accidentally regress a passing dimension. If 5 of 6 dimensions passed and only examples_distinguish_from_siblings failed, you may only need to swap one or two examples — do not rewrite the description.
user_template:
The previous problem draft failed its rubric. Revise it.

<principle_id>{{ principle_id }}</principle_id>

<aws_anchor>
  <best_practice>{{ aws_bp }}</best_practice>
  <implementation_step>
    <number>{{ aws_step_number }}</number>
    <title>{{ aws_step_title }}</title>
    <verbatim_text>{{ aws_step_verbatim }}</verbatim_text>
  </implementation_step>
</aws_anchor>

<statement>
  <title>{{ statement_title }}</title>
  <description>{{ statement_description }}</description>
</statement>

<siblings_under_same_bp>
{{ sibling_problems }}
</siblings_under_same_bp>

<failing_draft>
  <description>{{ candidate_description }}</description>
  <examples>{{ candidate_examples }}</examples>
</failing_draft>

<rubric_scores>
  <names_failure_mode score="{{ score_names_failure_mode }}">{{ justification_names_failure_mode }}</names_failure_mode>
  <failure_mechanism_explicit score="{{ score_failure_mechanism_explicit }}">{{ justification_failure_mechanism_explicit }}</failure_mechanism_explicit>
  <examples_are_concrete score="{{ score_examples_are_concrete }}">{{ justification_examples_are_concrete }}</examples_are_concrete>
  <examples_distinguish_from_siblings score="{{ score_examples_distinguish_from_siblings }}">{{ justification_examples_distinguish_from_siblings }}</examples_distinguish_from_siblings>
  <illustrative_signposting score="{{ score_illustrative_signposting }}">{{ justification_illustrative_signposting }}</illustrative_signposting>
  <parallel_form_with_siblings score="{{ score_parallel_form_with_siblings }}">{{ justification_parallel_form_with_siblings }}</parallel_form_with_siblings>
</rubric_scores>

Return the JSON object specified in the output contract — same shape as the generate output, drop-in replacement.

output_contract.note: Same shape as sections/problem/generate.json output_contract.schema — pipeline treats this as a drop-in replacement for the failing draft.
